findings.evidence: _check_grid_intersection already builds this set internally at line 210. Lines 774-775 rebuild it for the ledger append. Harmless duplication, not a bug.

findings.claim: Strict equality check operates on float accuracy/degradation rather than integer n_correct counts
findings.evidence: Carried forward from r3 PASS. abs(round(x,4) - round(y,4)) > 0 on floats is correct in practice because both conditions share an identical forward pass and baseline_acc. Comment says 'counts-based metrics' but implementation uses floats. Not a new regression.
findings.suggested_fix: Store n_correct in rows dict and compare integers directly for more robust semantics (deferred).
